mnemonics.title: Calibration Steps
mnemonics.content: T-R-A-C-E: Trace standards, Record measurements, Apply corrections, Check validation, Establish new baseline

mnemonics.title: Why Calibrate
mnemonics.content: A.C.C.U.R.A.T.E.: Accurate decisions, Cost savings, Capacity planning, Understanding true state, Reliable metrics, Alerts accuracy, Trouble-free operations, Equipment confidence

mnemonics.title: Calibration Types
mnemonics.content: Z.S.P.A.N.: Zero (offset), Span (sensitivity), Precision check, Accuracy verification, Non-linear adjustment

acronyms.expansion: National Institute of Standards and Technology
acronyms.usage: Primary standards body providing reference values for traceable calibration
Defines performance thresholds that require calibrated monitoring to verify compliance
Stores calibration records and baseline data for IT assets
Tools that require regular calibration to provide accurate performance metrics
Capacity planning metric that depends on accurate calibration of backup systems

visualTips.visualization: Think of a weighing scale: Zero sets the starting point (no weight), Span adjusts how much the needle moves for a given weight - like setting both endpoints of a ruler correctly
Draw archery targets: Accuracy = arrows near bullseye (true value), Precision = arrows clustered together (even if off-center)
Picture a clock that gains 1 minute every week - gradual drift from true time requiring periodic correction
Think of a relay race where each runner (calibration lab) passes the baton (measurement standard) with documented handoffs, showing unbroken connection to national standards

analogies.analogy: Like a musical instrument tuning - a piano needs tuning before a concert to ensure it plays the correct notes, just as IT systems need calibration to show correct performance readings
Like setting a thermostat to the correct room temperature - you first establish what 'normal' feels like so you can detect when it's too hot or cold
Like a car's odometer gradually showing wrong mileage due to tire wear changes - the measurement drifts from true value over time
Like car service intervals - more used cars need more frequent service, and instruments with higher drift rates need more frequent calibration
